manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: server, school, hood, alien, knife, pole, figure, tube, oil.
Your opponent's team words are: shoe, triangle, microscope, fish, turkey, whale, giant, strike.
Distractor words are: octopus, revolution, agent, club, sock, smuggler, cold.
The kill words are: bat.

Opponent's words: shoe, triangle, microscope, fish, turkey, whale, giant, strike

Distractors: octopus, revolution, agent, club, sock, smuggler, cold

"Well" connects to oil extraction. "Drill" could work for both oil and school contexts. "Liquid" and "Lubricant" both point to oil. For alien, words like "UFO," "Stranger," or "Foreign" work better than the too-obvious "Extraterrestrial." "Blade" and "Cutting" both relate to knife.

Moving through more possibilities: "Elementary," "High," and "Charter" all connect to school. "Old" only gives one link with "old school." "Car" might bridge multiple concepts through hood and oil, but it feels tenuous. "Test" and "Inner" don't generate enough strong connections. I should focus on finding clues that naturally connect multiple words rather than forcing weak connections. figure (essential figure?) - too weak.

"OLIVE", "BABY", "MOTOR", and "CRUDE" all work with oil but only give me single-word connections. "SKATING" pairs with figure, though pole doesn't fit well. "FATHER" similarly only connects to figure.

Going back to "STEEL" or "METAL" looks more promising - knife, pole, and tube all work as steel or metal variants, giving me a solid three-word match. Let me verify the distractor words don't have metal associations - shoe doesn't fit that pattern.

But checking further, bat could be a metal bat, and triangle could refer to the metal percussion instrument, which creates real risks. "COLD" as in "cold as steel" is too weak. This makes "METAL" problematic.

"HOLLOW" might work with tube and pole but only gives two connections. "CYLINDRICAL" or "CYLINDER" similarly feels limited to just tube.

"FISHING" connects to pole through fishing pole, but that's risky since fish is the opponent. "VAULTING" or "VAULT" works with pole vault but that's only one word.

Looking at "SCHOOL" - there are solid combinations like old school, school teacher, school bus, grade school, high school. And "TUBE" has test tube, inner tube, YouTube (though that's a brand name).

"TEST" seems stronger: test tube is a very direct association, and school test is equally strong. None of the opponent words connect well to test, though agent might be a weak link. This could work for at least two of my words.

I could also consider "CYLINDRICAL" for tube and pole, or stick with a cooking theme - "COOKING" with oil and knife, or "KITCHEN" with knife and oil. "METAL" could connect knife, pole, and tube but that feels riskier. "HOLLOW" works for tube and pole as a two-word option.

TEST seems like the safest bet for connecting tube and school clearly. Let me explore if there's anything stronger for three or more words. "GALACTIC" or "SPACE" don't quite work - alien and pole don't have a solid connection. "HIGH" mostly just applies to school, and "GRADE" is too weak for school and oil. "SERVING" could link server and knife, though the connection to knife is a bit loose.

I'm also considering "TABLE" - it works with both server and knife, giving me two solid connections. But I keep coming back to TEST as the most reliable option. I've checked petroleum, slick, boarding, prep, and graduate - they all only connect to one word. "MEDICAL" could pair with school and tube, but the tube connection feels forced.

SURGICAL stands out though - it genuinely links to both knife and tube. So I'm weighing TEST against SURGICAL as my top candidates, along with KITCHEN for knife and oil. TEST feels like the safest choice for connecting tube and school.
